tools.name: read_document
tools.description: Read a Google Doc and return its content as clean Markdown. Preserves headings, bold, italic, strikethrough, links, code spans, ordered/unordered lists with nesting, and tables. This is the standard way to read document content. For editing, use append_text, replace_all_text, first-match tools, or batch_update.

tools.name: create_document
tools.description: Create a new empty Google Doc with the given title. Returns the created document's metadata including its documentId, which is needed for all subsequent operations on the document.

tools.name: batch_update
tools.description: Send a documents.batchUpdate request to modify a document with one or more structured requests. Supports insertText, deleteContentRange, replaceAllText, createNamedRange, updateTextStyle, updateParagraphStyle, insertTable, insertInlineImage, and more. For common operations, prefer the higher-level tools: append_text, replace_all_text, and the first-match tools. Use batch_update for operations not covered by those helpers.

tools.name: append_text
tools.description: Append plain text to the end of a Google Doc. Automatically fetches the document to find the correct end index and inserts the text there. Use this for adding content to the end of a document without needing to know the document structure.

tools.name: replace_all_text
tools.description: Replace all occurrences of a text string in a Google Doc with new text. Case-sensitive by default. More efficient than the first-match tools when you need to replace every occurrence. Returns the number of occurrences replaced.

tools.name: style_first_match
tools.description: Find the first occurrence of text in a document and apply a TextStyle to it (bold, italic, fontSize, foregroundColor, etc.). Uses a marker-based approach: replaces the text with a unique marker, locates the marker's position, applies the style, then restores the original text. Returns {applied: true/false}.

tools.name: insert_text_after_first_match
tools.description: Find the first occurrence of text and insert new text immediately before or after it. Useful for inserting content at a specific anchor point in the document without knowing exact character indices. Returns {applied: true/false}.

tools.name: insert_table_after_first_match
tools.description: Find the first occurrence of text and insert a table with the specified number of rows and columns nearby. Returns {applied: true/false}.

tools.name: insert_page_break_after_first_match
tools.description: Find the first occurrence of text and insert a page break nearby. Useful for structuring long documents. Returns {applied: true/false}.

tools.name: insert_inline_image_after_first_match
tools.description: Find the first occurrence of text and insert an inline image nearby, referenced by URL. Returns {applied: true/false}.

tools.name: delete_first_match
tools.description: Find the first occurrence of text in the document and delete it. Only the first match is removed. Use replace_all_text with an empty string to remove all occurrences. Returns {applied: true/false}.

tools.name: update_paragraph_style_for_first_match
tools.description: Find the first occurrence of text and update the paragraph style for the paragraph containing it. Use to apply heading levels (HEADING_1 through HEADING_6), NORMAL_TEXT, or adjust spacing, alignment, and indentation. Returns {applied: true/false}.

tools.name: update_document_style
tools.description: Update document-level style properties such as page size (pageSize.width, pageSize.height in pt), margins (marginTop, marginBottom, marginLeft, marginRight in pt), and page orientation. Does not affect individual paragraph or text styles.
